name: Ruth's Peppermint Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
Candy should be finely crushed
1. Stir together the flour and salt.
2. Beat margarine or butter in a mixer bowl for 30 seconds.
3. Beat in powdered sugar until fluffy. Add egg, vanilla, and extract; beat well. Add the dry ingredients. Beat until just combined. Divide dough into half.
4. Mix food coloring into one portion of dough. Chill dough for 1 hour or until easy to handle.
5. Divide each portion of dough into half. On lightly floured surface, roll out each of the four balls of dough to form an 8-inch square.
6. Place a white square of cookie dough on top of a red square of cookie dough. Roll up in a jelly roll style. Repeat with remaining dough.
7. Wrap rolls in waxed paper and chill for 2 to 24 hours.
8. Cut dough into 1/4-inch thick slices. Place on ungreased cookie sheets.
9. Bake in a 375°F oven for 8 to 10 minutes or until edges are firm and bottoms are light brown. Remove cookies and cool on wire racks.
10. Beat together egg white and water; brush over warm cookies. Sprinkle with crushed peppermint candy.
